name: Ubume
description: Ubume is a Japanese yōkai, specifically a type of ghost of a woman who died in childbirth. She is often depicted as a sorrowful figure, eternally searching for her lost child or attempting to care for a living child as a surrogate. Her appearance is typically that of a woman covered in blood, often with a partially decomposed or rotting body. She may carry a child, or the child may be invisible, represented only by the sounds of its crying. Ubume are known for their desperate pleas for help and their attempts to lure people into dangerous situations, often related to water or childbirth. They are a poignant reminder of the dangers and tragedies associated with childbirth in pre-modern Japan, embodying themes of maternal grief, loss, and the lingering pain of unfinished business. Some tales tell of Ubume buying sweets for children, paying with dead leaves or worthless objects. The legends surrounding Ubume serve as warnings against neglecting or mistreating pregnant women and new mothers, highlighting the importance of proper care and respect during this vulnerable time. The Ubume's cries are said to be particularly haunting and unsettling, capable of driving listeners to madness or despair. She is a symbol of the fragility of life and the enduring power of maternal instinct, even beyond death.
subtitle: The Ghost of a Mother's Grief
appearance: A woman covered in blood, often with a decomposing or rotting lower body. She wears tattered clothes and has long, disheveled hair. Sometimes carries a child, either living or a ghostly apparition.
habitat: Near rivers, cemeteries, or places where women have died in childbirth. Often seen at night, near populated areas.
behavior: Desperately searches for her lost child or attempts to care for other children. Pleads for help from passersby, often leading them into danger. Emits haunting cries and wails.

keyMyths.title: The Ubume's Candy
keyMyths.description: An Ubume attempts to buy candy for a child with dead leaves. When the shopkeeper discovers the deception, the Ubume disappears, leaving behind only a pool of blood.

keyMyths.title: The River's Call
keyMyths.description: An Ubume lures people to a riverbank with her cries, hoping they will care for her spectral child. Those who follow her voice often drown or meet other unfortunate ends.
keyMyths.source: Kwaidan: Stories and Studies of Strange Things by Lafcadio Hearn

sources.author: Lafcadio Hearn
sources.work: Kwaidan: Stories and Studies of Strange Things
sources.description: A collection of Japanese folk tales and ghost stories, including some featuring Ubume.
sources.text: A collection of Japanese folk tales and ghost stories, including some featuring Ubume.
